modules.name: unittest2.test.dummy
documentation: Empty module for testing the loading of modules

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_countTestCases
documentation: "Return the number of tests represented by the this test object. For
TestCase instances, this will always be 1"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_setUp
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if setUp() raises
an exception.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_test
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if the test raises
an error (as opposed to a failure).

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_run_call_order__failure_in_test
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if the test signals
a failure (as opposed to an error).

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_tearDown
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if tearDown() raises
an exception.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_id
documentation: "Return a string identifying the specific test case."
Because of the vague nature of the docs, I'm not going to lock this
test down too much. Really all that can be asserted is that the id()
will be a string (either 8-byte or unicode -- again, because the docs
just say "string")

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_shortDescription__no_docstring
documentation: "Returns a one-line description of the test, or None if no description
has been provided. The default implementation of this method returns
the first line of the test method's docstring, if available, or None."

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_functiontestcase.Test_FunctionTestCase.test_shortDescription__singleline_docstring
documentation: "Returns a one-line description of the test, or None if no description
has been provided. The default implementation of this method returns
the first line of the test method's docstring, if available, or None."

classes.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test
documentation: ## Support code for Test_TestSuite

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_init__tests_optional
documentation: ## /Set up attributes needed by inherited tests
## Tests for TestSuite.__init__
"class TestSuite([tests])"
The tests iterable should be optional

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_init__empty_tests
documentation: "class TestSuite([tests])"
"If tests is given, it must be an iterable of individual test cases
or other test suites that will be used to build the suite initially"
TestSuite should deal with empty tests iterables by allowing the
creation of an empty suite

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_init__tests_from_any_iterable
documentation: "class TestSuite([tests])"
"If tests is given, it must be an iterable of individual test cases
or other test suites that will be used to build the suite initially"
TestSuite should allow any iterable to provide tests

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_init__TestSuite_instances_in_tests
documentation: "class TestSuite([tests])"
"If tests is given, it must be an iterable of individual test cases
or other test suites that will be used to build the suite initially"
Does TestSuite() also allow other TestSuite() instances to be present
in the tests iterable?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_iter
documentation: ## /Tests for TestSuite.__init__
Container types should support the iter protocol

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_countTestCases_zero_simple
documentation: "Return the number of tests represented by the this test object.
...this method is also implemented by the TestSuite class, which can
return larger [greater than 1] values"
Presumably an empty TestSuite returns 0?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_countTestCases_zero_nested
documentation: "Return the number of tests represented by the this test object.
...this method is also implemented by the TestSuite class, which can
return larger [greater than 1] values"
Presumably an empty TestSuite (even if it contains other empty
TestSuite instances) returns 0?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_countTestCases_simple
documentation: "Return the number of tests represented by the this test object.
...this method is also implemented by the TestSuite class, which can
return larger [greater than 1] values"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_countTestCases_nested
documentation: "Return the number of tests represented by the this test object.
...this method is also implemented by the TestSuite class, which can
return larger [greater than 1] values"
Make sure this holds for nested TestSuite instances, too

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_run__empty_suite
documentation: "Run the tests associated with this suite, collecting the result into
the test result object passed as result."
And if there are no tests? What then?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_run__requires_result
documentation: "Note that unlike TestCase.run(), TestSuite.run() requires the
"result object to be passed in."

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_run
documentation: "Run the tests associated with this suite, collecting the result into
the test result object passed as result."

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_addTest__TestCase
documentation: "Add a TestCase ... to the suite"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_addTest__TestSuite
documentation: "Add a ... TestSuite to the suite"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_addTests
documentation: "Add all the tests from an iterable of TestCase and TestSuite
instances to this test suite."
"This is equivalent to iterating over tests, calling addTest() for
each element"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_suite.Test_TestSuite.test_addTest__noniterable
documentation: "Add all the tests from an iterable of TestCase and TestSuite
instances to this test suite."
What happens if it doesn't get an iterable?

classes.name: unittest2.test.test_assertions.TestLongMessage
documentation:
Test that the individual asserts honour longMessage.
This actually tests all the message behaviour for
asserts that use longMessage.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromTestCase
documentation: ## Tests for TestLoader.loadTestsFromTestCase
"Return a suite of all tests cases contained in the TestCase-derived
class testCaseClass"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromTestCase__no_matches
documentation: "Return a suite of all tests cases contained in the TestCase-derived
class testCaseClass"
Make sure it does the right thing even if no tests were found

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromTestCase__TestSuite_subclass
documentation: "Return a suite of all tests cases contained in the TestCase-derived
class testCaseClass"
What happens if loadTestsFromTestCase() is given an object
that isn't a subclass of TestCase? Specifically, what happens
if testCaseClass is a subclass of TestSuite?
This is checked for specifically in the code, so we better add a
test for it.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromTestCase__default_method_name
documentation: "Return a suite of all tests cases contained in the TestCase-derived
class testCaseClass"
Make sure loadTestsFromTestCase() picks up the default test method
name (as specified by TestCase), even though the method name does
not match the default TestLoader.testMethodPrefix string

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromModule__TestCase_subclass
documentation: ## /Tests for TestLoader.loadTestsFromTestCase
## Tests for TestLoader.loadTestsFromModule
"This method searches `module` for classes derived from TestCase"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromModule__no_TestCase_instances
documentation: "This method searches `module` for classes derived from TestCase"
What happens if no tests are found (no TestCase instances)?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromModule__no_TestCase_tests
documentation: "This method searches `module` for classes derived from TestCase"
What happens if no tests are found (TestCases instances, but no tests)?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromModule__not_a_module
documentation: "This method searches `module` for classes derived from TestCase"s
What happens if loadTestsFromModule() is given something other
than a module?
XXX Currently, it succeeds anyway. This flexibility
should either be documented or loadTestsFromModule() should
raise a TypeError
XXX Certain people are using this behaviour. We'll add a test for it

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromModule__load_tests
documentation: Check that loadTestsFromModule honors (or not) a module
with a load_tests function.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__empty_name
documentation: ## /Tests for TestLoader.loadTestsFromModule()
## Tests for TestLoader.loadTestsFromName()
"The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
Is ValueError raised in response to an empty name?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__malformed_name
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
What happens when the name contains invalid characters?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__unknown_module_name
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve ... to a
module"
What happens when a module by that name can't be found?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__unknown_attr_name
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
What happens when the module is found, but the attribute can't?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_unknown_name
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
What happens when we provide the module, but the attribute can't be
found?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_empty_name
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
"The method optionally resolves name relative to the given module"
Does loadTestsFromName raise ValueError when passed an empty
name relative to a provided module?
XXX Should probably raise a ValueError instead of an AttributeError

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_malformed_name
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
"The method optionally resolves name relative to the given module"
What happens when an impossible name is given, relative to the provided
`module`?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_not_a_module
documentation: "The method optionally resolves name relative to the given module"
Does loadTestsFromName raise TypeError when the `module` argument
isn't a module object?
XXX Accepts the not-a-module object, ignorning the object's type
This should raise an exception or the method name should be changed
XXX Some people are relying on this, so keep it for now

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_bad_object
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
Does it raise an exception if the name resolves to an invalid
object?

documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may
resolve either to ... a test case class"

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_invalid_testmethod
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to
a module, a test case class, a TestSuite instance, a test method
within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a
TestCase or TestSuite instance."
Does loadTestsFromName() raise the proper exception when trying to

Override the suiteClass attribute to ensure that the suiteClass

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromName__relative_testmethod_ProperSuiteClass
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve ... to

"Similar to loadTestsFromName(), but takes a sequence of names rather
than a single name."
What happens if that sequence of names is empty?

than a single name."

What happens if that sequence of names is empty?
XXX Should this raise a ValueError or just return an empty TestSuite?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_loadTestsFromNames__unknown_name_relative_2
documentation: "The specifier name is a ``dotted name'' that may resolve either to

within a test case class, or a callable object which returns a

XXX This currently raises AttributeError, though ValueError is probably
more appropriate

documentation: "The method optionally resolves name relative to the given module"
Does loadTestsFromNames() make sure the provided `module` is in fact

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_getTestCaseNames
documentation: ## /Tests for TestLoader.loadTestsFromNames()
## Tests for TestLoader.getTestCaseNames()
"Return a sorted sequence of method names found within testCaseClass"
Test.foobar is defined to make sure getTestCaseNames() respects
loader.testMethodPrefix

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_getTestCaseNames__no_tests
documentation: "Return a sorted sequence of method names found within testCaseClass"
Does getTestCaseNames() behave appropriately if no tests are found?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_getTestCaseNames__not_a_TestCase
documentation: "Return a sorted sequence of method names found within testCaseClass"
Are not-TestCases handled gracefully?
XXX This should raise a TypeError, not return a list
XXX It's too late in the 2.5 release cycle to fix this, but it should
probably be revisited for 2.6

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_getTestCaseNames__inheritance
documentation: "Return a sorted sequence of method names found within testCaseClass"
Make sure inherited names are handled.
TestP.foobar is defined to make sure getTestCaseNames() respects
loader.testMethodPrefix

documentation: ## /Tests for TestLoader.getTestCaseNames()
## Tests for TestLoader.testMethodPrefix
"String giving the prefix of method names which will be interpreted as
test methods"
Implicit in the documentation is that testMethodPrefix is respected by
all loadTestsFrom* methods.

documentation: "String giving the prefix of method names which will be interpreted as
test methods"

all loadTestsFrom* methods.

Does it actually affect getTestCaseNames()?

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_sortTestMethodsUsing__default_value
documentation: "The default value is the built-in cmp() function"
Since cmp is now defunct, we simply verify that the results
occur in the same order as they would with the default sort.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_loader.Test_TestLoader.test_sortTestMethodsUsing__None
documentation: "it can be set to None to disable the sort."
XXX How is this different from reassigning cmp? Are the tests returned
in a random order or something? This behaviour should die

documentation: ## /Tests for TestLoader.sortTestMethodsUsing

functions.name: unittest2.test.support._filterwarnings

Catch the warnings, then check if all the expected
warnings have been raised and re-raise unexpected warnings.
If 'quiet' is True, only re-raise the unexpected warnings.

functions.name: unittest2.test.support.check_warnings
documentation:
Context manager to silence warnings.

Accept 2-tuples as positional arguments:
    ("message regexp", WarningCategory)

Optional argument:
 - if 'quiet' is True, it does not fail if a filter catches nothing
    (default True without argument,
     default False if some filters are defined)

Without argument, it defaults to:
    check_warnings(("", Warning), quiet=True)

A simple test suite that doesn't provide class or module shared fixtures.

classes.name: unittest2.suite.TestSuite

methods.name: unittest2.suite.TestSuite.debug
documentation: Run the tests without collecting errors in a TestResult

methods.name: unittest2.suite.TestSuite._wrapped_run
documentation: private methods

A test suite is a composite test consisting of a number of TestCases.

For use, create an instance of TestSuite, then add test case instances.
When all tests have been added, the suite can be passed to a test
runner, such as TextTestRunner. It will run the individual test cases
in the order in which they were added, aggregating the results. When
subclassing, do not forget to call the base class constructor.

classes.name: unittest2.suite._ErrorHolder
documentation:
Placeholder for a TestCase inside a result. As far as a TestResult
is concerned, this looks exactly like a unit test. Used to insert
arbitrary errors into a test suite run.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_discovery.TestDiscovery.test_get_name_from_path
documentation: Heavily mocked tests so I can avoid hitting the filesystem

modules.name: unittest2
documentation:
Python unit testing framework, based on Erich Gamma's JUnit and Kent Beck's
Smalltalk testing framework.

This module contains the core framework classes that form the basis of
specific test cases and suites (TestCase, TestSuite etc.), and also a
text-based utility class for running the tests and reporting the results
 (TextTestRunner).

Simple usage:

    import unittest2

    class IntegerArithmenticTestCase(unittest2.TestCase):
        def testAdd(self):  ## test method names begin 'test*'
            self.assertEqual((1 + 2), 3)
            self.assertEqual(0 + 1, 1)
        def testMultiply(self):
            self.assertEqual((0 * 10), 0)
            self.assertEqual((5 * 8), 40)

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        unittest2.main()

Further information is available in the bundled documentation, and from

  http://docs.python.org/library/unittest.html

Copyright (c) 1999-2003 Steve Purcell
Copyright (c) 2003-2010 Python Software Foundation
This module is free software, and you may redistribute it and/or modify
it under the same terms as Python itself, so long as this copyright message
and disclaimer are retained in their original form.

IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHOR BE LIABLE TO ANY PARTY FOR DIRECT, INDIRECT,
SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THE USE OF
THIS CODE, EVEN IF THE AUTHOR HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH
DAMAGE.

THE AUTHOR SPECIFICALLY DISCLAIMS ANY WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT
LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A
PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  THE CODE PROVIDED HEREUNDER IS ON AN "AS IS" BASIS,
AND THERE IS NO OBLIGATION WHATSOEVER TO PROVIDE MAINTENANCE,
SUPPORT, UPDATES, ENHANCEMENTS, OR MODIFICATIONS.

modules.name: unittest2.main
documentation: Unittest main program

classes.name: unittest2.main.TestProgram
documentation:
A command-line program that runs a set of tests; this is primarily
for making test modules conveniently executable.

modules.name: unittest2.util
documentation: Various utility functions.

functions.name: unittest2.util.sorted_list_difference
documentation:
Finds elements in only one or the other of two, sorted input lists.

Returns a two-element tuple of lists.    The first list contains those
elements in the "expected" list but not in the "actual" list, and the
second contains those elements in the "actual" list but not in the
"expected" list.    Duplicate elements in either input list are ignored.

functions.name: unittest2.util.unorderable_list_difference
documentation:
Same behavior as sorted_list_difference but
for lists of unorderable items (like dicts).

As it does a linear search per item (remove) it
has O(n*n) performance.

functions.name: unittest2.util.three_way_cmp
documentation: Return -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y and 1 if x > y

functions.name: unittest2.compatibility.cmp_to_key
documentation: Convert a cmp= function into a key= function
Python 3.0 / 3.1

documentation: Main entry point

modules.name: unittest2.runner
documentation: Running tests

classes.name: unittest2.runner._WritelnDecorator
documentation: Used to decorate file-like objects with a handy 'writeln' method

classes.name: unittest2.runner.TextTestResult
documentation:
A test result class that can print formatted text results to a stream.

Used by TextTestRunner.

classes.name: unittest2.runner.TextTestRunner
documentation:
A test runner class that displays results in textual form.

It prints out the names of tests as they are run, errors as they
occur, and a summary of the results at the end of the test run.

modules.name: unittest2.loader
documentation: Loading unittests.

classes.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader.loadTestsFromTestCase
documentation: Return a suite of all tests cases contained in testCaseClass

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader.loadTestsFromModule
documentation: Return a suite of all tests cases contained in the given module

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader.loadTestsFromName
documentation:
Return a suite of all tests cases given a string specifier.

The name may resolve either to a module, a test case class, a
test method within a test case class, or a callable object which
returns a TestCase or TestSuite instance.

The method optionally resolves the names relative to a given module.

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader.loadTestsFromNames
documentation:
Return a suite of all tests cases found using the given sequence
of string specifiers. See 'loadTestsFromName()'.

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader.getTestCaseNames
documentation: Return a sorted sequence of method names found within testCaseClass

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader.discover
documentation:
Find and return all test modules from the specified start
directory, recursing into subdirectories to find them. Only test files
that match the pattern will be loaded. (Using shell style pattern
matching.)

All test modules must be importable from the top level of the project.
If the start directory is not the top level directory then the top
level directory must be specified separately.

If a test package name (directory with '__init__.py') matches the
pattern then the package will be checked for a 'load_tests' function. If
this exists then it will be called with loader, tests, pattern.

If load_tests exists then discovery does  *not* recurse into the package,
load_tests is responsible for loading all tests in the package.

The pattern is deliberately not stored as a loader attribute so that
packages can continue discovery themselves. top_level_dir is stored so
load_tests does not need to pass this argument in to loader.discover().

methods.name: unittest2.loader.TestLoader._find_tests
documentation: Used by discovery. Yields test suites it loads.

This class is responsible for loading tests according to various criteria
and returning them wrapped in a TestSuite

Called when an error has occurred. 'err' is a tuple of values as

methods.name: unittest2.result.TestResult._exc_info_to_string
documentation: Converts a sys.exc_info()-style tuple of values into a string.

Holder for test result information.

Test results are automatically managed by the TestCase and TestSuite
classes, and do not need to be explicitly manipulated by writers of tests.

Each instance holds the total number of tests run, and collections of
failures and errors that occurred among those test runs. The collections
contain tuples of (testcase, exceptioninfo), where exceptioninfo is the
formatted traceback of the error that occurred.

method named methodName."

TestCase instances, this will always be 1"

test. For TestCase instances, this will always be

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_setUp
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if setUp() raises
an exception.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_setUp_default_result
documentation: "With a temporary result stopTestRun is called when setUp errors.

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_test
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if the test raises
an error (as opposed to a failure).

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_test_default_result
documentation: "With a default result, an error in the test still results in stopTestRun
being called."

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__failure_in_test

prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if the test signals
a failure (as opposed to an error).

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__failure_in_test_default_result
documentation: "When a test fails with a default result stopTestRun is still called."

methods.name: unittest2.test.test_case.Test_TestCase.test_run_call_order__error_in_tearDown
documentation: "When a setUp() method is defined, the test runner will run that method
prior to each test. Likewise, if a tearDown() method is defined, the
test runner will invoke that method after each test. In the example,
setUp() was used to create a fresh sequence for each test."
Make sure the proper call order is maintained, even if tearDown() raises
an exception.

documentation: "When tearDown errors with a default result stopTestRun is still called."

that does not support startTestRun and stopTestRun.

If a test framework needs to use a specialized exception, possibly to

This test confirms their continued existence and functionality
in order to avoid breaking existing code.

modules.name: unittest2.case
documentation: Test case implementation

functions.name: unittest2.case.skip
documentation: Unconditionally skip a test.

functions.name: unittest2.case.skipIf
documentation: Skip a test if the condition is true.

functions.name: unittest2.case.skipUnless
documentation: Skip a test unless the condition is true.

classes.name: unittest2.case.SkipTest
documentation:
Raise this exception in a test to skip it.

Usually you can use TestResult.skip() or one of the skipping decorators
instead of raising this directly.

classes.name: unittest2.case._ExpectedFailure
documentation:
Raise this when a test is expected to fail.

This is an implementation detail.

classes.name: unittest2.case._UnexpectedSuccess
documentation: The test was supposed to fail, but it didn't!

classes.name: unittest2.case._AssertRaisesContext
documentation: A context manager used to implement TestCase.assertRaises* methods.

classes.name: unittest2.case.TestCase

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.__init__
documentation:
Create an instance of the class that will use the named test
method when executed. Raises a ValueError if the instance does
not have a method with the specified name.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.addTypeEqualityFunc
documentation:
Add a type specific assertEqual style function to compare a type.

This method is for use by TestCase subclasses that need to register
their own type equality functions to provide nicer error messages.

Args:
    typeobj: The data type to call this function on when both values
            are of the same type in assertEqual().
    function: The callable taking two arguments and an optional
            msg= argument that raises self.failureException with a
            useful error message when the two arguments are not equal.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.addCleanup
documentation:
Add a function, with arguments, to be called when the test is
completed. Functions added are called on a LIFO basis and are
called after tearDown on test failure or success.

Cleanup items are called even if setUp fails (unlike tearDown).

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.shortDescription
documentation:
Returns a one-line description of the test, or None if no
description has been provided.

The default implementation of this method returns the first line of
the specified test method's docstring.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.doCleanups
documentation:
Execute all cleanup functions. Normally called for you after
tearDown.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.debug
documentation: Run the test without collecting errors in a TestResult

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.skipTest
documentation: Skip this test.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.fail
documentation: Fail immediately, with the given message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertTrue
documentation: Fail the test unless the expression is true.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase._formatMessage
documentation:
Honour the longMessage attribute when generating failure messages.
If longMessage is False this means:
* Use only an explicit message if it is provided
* Otherwise use the standard message for the assert

If longMessage is True:
* Use the standard message
* If an explicit message is provided, plus ' : ' and the explicit message

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertRaises
documentation:
Fail unless an exception of class excClass is thrown
by callableObj when invoked with arguments args and keyword
arguments kwargs. If a different type of exception is
thrown, it will not be caught, and the test case will be
deemed to have suffered an error, exactly as for an
unexpected exception.

If called with callableObj omitted or None, will return a
context object used like this:

```
with self.assertRaises(SomeException):
    do_something()
```

The context manager keeps a reference to the exception as
the 'exception' attribute. This allows you to inspect the
exception after the assertion:

```
with self.assertRaises(SomeException) as cm:
    do_something()
the_exception = cm.exception
self.assertEqual(the_exception.error_code, 3)
```

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase._getAssertEqualityFunc
documentation:
Get a detailed comparison function for the types of the two args.

Returns: A callable accepting (first, second, msg=None) that will
raise a failure exception if first != second with a useful human
readable error message for those types.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase._baseAssertEqual
documentation: The default assertEqual implementation, not type specific.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertEqual
documentation:
Fail if the two objects are unequal as determined by the '=='
operator.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertNotEqual
documentation:
Fail if the two objects are equal as determined by the '=='
operator.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertAlmostEqual
documentation:
Fail if the two objects are unequal as determined by their
difference rounded to the given number of decimal places
(default 7) and comparing to zero, or by comparing that the
between the two objects is more than the given delta.

Note that decimal places (from zero) are usually not the same
as significant digits (measured from the most signficant digit).

If the two objects compare equal then they will automatically
compare almost equal.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertNotAlmostEqual
documentation:
Fail if the two objects are equal as determined by their
difference rounded to the given number of decimal places
(default 7) and comparing to zero, or by comparing that the
between the two objects is less than the given delta.

Note that decimal places (from zero) are usually not the same
as significant digits (measured from the most signficant digit).

Objects that are equal automatically fail.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase._deprecate
documentation: These fail* assertion method names are pending deprecation and will
be a DeprecationWarning in 3.2; http://bugs.python.org/issue2578

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertSequenceEqual
documentation:
An equality assertion for ordered sequences (like lists and tuples).

For the purposes of this function, a valid ordered sequence type is one
which can be indexed, has a length, and has an equality operator.

Args:
    seq1: The first sequence to compare.
    seq2: The second sequence to compare.
    seq_type: The expected datatype of the sequences, or None if no
            datatype should be enforced.
    msg: Optional message to use on failure instead of a list of
            differences.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertListEqual
documentation:
A list-specific equality assertion.

Args:
    list1: The first list to compare.
    list2: The second list to compare.
    msg: Optional message to use on failure instead of a list of
            differences.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertTupleEqual
documentation:
A tuple-specific equality assertion.

Args:
    tuple1: The first tuple to compare.
    tuple2: The second tuple to compare.
    msg: Optional message to use on failure instead of a list of
            differences.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertSetEqual
documentation:
A set-specific equality assertion.

Args:
    set1: The first set to compare.
    set2: The second set to compare.
    msg: Optional message to use on failure instead of a list of
            differences.

assertSetEqual uses ducktyping to support different types of sets, and
is optimized for sets specifically (parameters must support a
difference method).

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertIn
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a in b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertNotIn
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a not in b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertIs
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a is b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertIsNot
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a is not b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertDictContainsSubset
documentation: Checks whether actual is a superset of expected.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertSameElements
documentation:
An unordered sequence specific comparison.

Raises with an error message listing which elements of expected_seq
are missing from actual_seq and vice versa if any.

Duplicate elements are ignored when comparing *expected_seq* and
*actual_seq*. It is the equivalent of `assertEqual(set(expected),
set(actual))` but it works with sequences of unhashable objects as
well.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertItemsEqual
documentation:
An unordered sequence / set specific comparison. It asserts that
expected_seq and actual_seq contain the same elements. It is
the equivalent of:

```
self.assertEqual(sorted(expected_seq), sorted(actual_seq))
```

Raises with an error message listing which elements of expected_seq
are missing from actual_seq and vice versa if any.

Asserts that each element has the same count in both sequences.
Example:

> - [0, 1, 1] and [1, 0, 1] compare equal.
> - [0, 0, 1] and [0, 1] compare unequal.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertMultiLineEqual
documentation: Assert that two multi-line strings are equal.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertLess
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a < b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertLessEqual
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a <= b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertGreater
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a > b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertGreaterEqual
documentation: Just like self.assertTrue(a >= b), but with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertIsNone
documentation: Same as self.assertTrue(obj is None), with a nicer default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertIsNotNone
documentation: Included for symmetry with assertIsNone.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertIsInstance
documentation:
Same as self.assertTrue(isinstance(obj, cls)), with a nicer
default message.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertNotIsInstance
documentation: Included for symmetry with assertIsInstance.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertRaisesRegexp
documentation:
Asserts that the message in a raised exception matches a regexp.

Args:
    expected_exception: Exception class expected to be raised.
    expected_regexp: Regexp (re pattern object or string) expected
            to be found in error message.
    callable_obj: Function to be called.
    args: Extra args.
    kwargs: Extra kwargs.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertRegexpMatches
documentation: Fail the test unless the text matches the regular expression.

methods.name: unittest2.case.TestCase.assertNotRegexpMatches
documentation: Fail the test if the text matches the regular expression.

A class whose instances are single test cases.

By default, the test code itself should be placed in a method named
'runTest'.

If the fixture may be used for many test cases, create as
many test methods as are needed. When instantiating such a TestCase
subclass, specify in the constructor arguments the name of the test method
that the instance is to execute.

Test authors should subclass TestCase for their own tests. Construction
and deconstruction of the test's environment ('fixture') can be
implemented by overriding the 'setUp' and 'tearDown' methods respectively.

If it is necessary to override the __init__ method, the base class
__init__ method must always be called. It is important that subclasses
should not change the signature of their __init__ method, since instances
of the classes are instantiated automatically by parts of the framework
in order to be run.

classes.name: unittest2.case.FunctionTestCase
documentation:
A test case that wraps a test function.

This is useful for slipping pre-existing test functions into the
unittest framework. Optionally, set-up and tidy-up functions can be
supplied. As with TestCase, the tidy-up ('tearDown') function will
always be called if the set-up ('setUp') function ran successfully.
